patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by escaping the forward slash ('/') character in Java strings, which is unnecessary according to Java string rules. We need to modify the methods to prevent escaping the forward slash when processing Java strings.

src/java/org/apache/commons/lang/StringE.original_code:
// Java and JavaScript //-------------------------------------------------------------------------- /** *

Escapes the characters in a String using Java String rules.

* *

Deals correctly with quotes and control-chars (tab, backslash, cr, ff, etc.)

* *

So a tab becomes the characters '\\' and * 't'.

* *

The only difference between Java strings and JavaScript strings * is that in JavaScript, a single quote must be escaped.

* *

Example: * 

     * input string: He didn't say, "Stop!"
     * output string: He didn't say, \"Stop!\"
     *
*

* * @param str String to escape values in, may be null * @return String with escaped values, null if null string input */ public static String escapeJava(String str) { return escapeJavaStyleString(str, false); }

/** *

Escapes the characters in a String using Java String rules to * a Writer.

* *

A null string input has no effect.

* * @see #escapeJava(java.lang.String) * @param out Writer to write escaped string into * @param str String to escape values in, may be null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the Writer is null * @throws IOException if error occurs on underlying Writer */ public static void escapeJava(Writer out, String str) throws IOException { escapeJavaStyleString(out, str, false); }

/** *

Escapes the characters in a String using JavaScript String rules.

*

Escapes any values it finds into their JavaScript String form. * Deals correctly with quotes and control-chars (tab, backslash, cr, ff, etc.)

* *

So a tab becomes the characters '\\' and * 't'.

* *

The only difference between Java strings and JavaScript strings * is that in JavaScript, a single quote must be escaped.

* *

Example: * 

     * input string: He didn't say, "Stop!"
     * output string: He didn\'t say, \"Stop!\"
     *
*

* * @param str String to escape values in, may be null * @return String with escaped values, null if null string input */ public static String escapeJavaScript(String str) { return escapeJavaStyleString(str, true); }

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the incorrect escaping of the forward slash ('/') character in the Java escape method. The forward slash should not be escaped in Java strings. We need to modify the escape logic to avoid escaping the forward slash unless specified.

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the incorrect escaping of the forward slash ('/') character in the Java string escaping methods. The forward slash should not be escaped in Java strings according to the Java language specification. We need to modify the methods to prevent escaping the forward slash.
